news.headline: Safe Pro Group to showcase Drone AI-Powered SpotlightAI technology at XPONENTIAL Europe in Düsseldorf from Feb 18-20, 2025.
news.summary: AVENTURA, Florida- Safe Pro Group Inc. (Nasdaq: SPAI), a prominent artificial intelligence (AI) solutions provider focusing on drone imagery processing, shared news that its Safe Pro AI subsidiary is set to showcase its patented Drone AI-Powered SpotlightAITM technology at XPONENTIAL Europe. This technology presentation will be featured at the renowned European trade fair for autonomous technologies and robotics, scheduled to happen in Düsseldorf, Germany from February 18th to February 20th, 2025. This event will serve as a platform for Safe Pro AI to exhibit its innovative solution to a wide audience of industry professionals and enthusiasts.

news.headline: Soluna Holdings, Inc. exits ERCOT planning for Project Kati, paving the way for 166 MW of renewable energy capacity.
news.summary: Soluna Holdings, Inc., a company listed on NASDAQ as SLNH, has completed the planning stage with Electric Reliability Council of Texas (ERCOT) for Project Kati. This project, aimed at developing green data centers for demanding computing tasks like Bitcoin mining and AI, is now set for construction and growth following this crucial milestone. Project Kati is set to provide a renewable energy capacity of up to 166 MW.

news.headline: Apex's 100 MW Great Kiskadee Storage in Texas begins commercial operations, aiding grid reliability and wildlife conservation efforts.
news.summary: Commercial operations have commenced at the Great Kiskadee Storage facility in Texas, developed by Apex with a capacity of 100 MW. This launch aims to bolster grid reliability while also contributing to wildlife conservation efforts in the region.

news.headline: GNFZ partners with OTEC in Brazil to advance net zero buildings, expanding certification services in Latin America.
news.summary: A strategic partnership between the Global Network for Zero (GNFZ) and OTEC, Brazil's leading sustainability consulting firm, was announced today in Washington. This collaboration signifies a major advancement in net zero certification services in Latin America. OTEC is now recognized as the inaugural GNFZ Partner in Brazil, forming a robust alliance to promote sustainable construction practices in the region. Brazil, known for its pioneering role in sustainable building initiatives, has emerged as a key player in driving this progress. The partnership is set to enhance efforts to foster environmentally-friendly building standards across the nation.

news.headline: Shore Capital partners with TCG Services, LLC, a facility management provider based in Kansas, with a network of vendor partners.
news.summary: In a recent development, Shore Capital Partners revealed its collaboration with TCG Services, LLC, an asset-lite company located in El Dorado, Kansas. TCG Services offers facility management solutions and operates via a vast network of vendor partners nationwide. Specializing in break-fix, preventative repair, and maintenance services, TCG caters to diverse multi-site clients within sectors such as food & beverage, retail, and healthcare. This partnership signifies a significant step for both companies, aiming to enhance their service offerings and expand their reach in the market.

news.headline: Slate Grocery REIT declares U.S.$0.072 distribution per Class U unit for Feb 2025, U.S.$0.864 annualized basis. Holders may opt for CAD.
news.summary: In Toronto, Slate Grocery REIT (TSX: SGR.U) declared a distribution for February 2025 at U.S.$0.072 per Class U unit, totalling U.S.$0.864 annually. Holders of Class U Units have the option to receive distributions in Canadian dollars by contacting their broker. The REIT specializes in U.S. grocery-anchored real estate and operates with the Board of Trustees overseeing these declarations.

news.headline: Evolus (NASDAQ: EOLS) will report Q4 and year end 2024 results on March 4, 2025, after US markets close. Conference call at 4:30pm ET.
news.summary: A performance beauty company, Evolus, Inc. (NASDAQ: EOLS), based in Newport Beach, California, revealed its plan to disclose its financial performance for the fourth quarter and the full year of 2024. The report is scheduled for Tuesday, March 4, 2025, after the closure of U.S. financial markets. In conjunction with this, Evolus management will conduct a conference call and live webcast on the same day at 4:30 p.m. ET. The call will be followed by a Q&A session where further insights may be provided.

news.headline: Paramount+ and EverPass Media partner to bring CBS Sports’ UEFA Champions League matches to commercial businesses starting Feb 18, 2025.
news.summary: In New York today, Paramount+ and EverPass Media revealed a long-term distribution deal. This agreement enables EverPass customers to livestream all CBS Sports’ UEFA Champions League games available on Paramount+. The streaming will kick off on Tuesday, February 18, 2025. This collaboration introduces a fresh means of accessing CBS Sports’ exclusive UEFA Champions League content, previously not accessible.

news.headline: Cognyte appoints 2 new Board members in Israel to boost expertise and growth.
news.summary: Herzliya, Israel-based company Cognyte has recently announced the addition of two new members to its Board of Directors, aiming to enhance their expertise and facilitate further growth within the organization.

news.headline: Study by Christian & Timbers finds gender disparities in leadership among PE & VC-backed companies, with 205 analyzed.
news.summary: In San Francisco, a recent study by Christian and Timbers highlights gender disparities in executive positions at private equity (PE) and venture capital (VC) backed companies. The analysis of 205 companies shows significant gaps in female representation across key roles like CEO, CFO, and CMO. The study suggests untapped potential for growth in female leadership within these sectors. The data indicates a need for increased focus on promoting gender diversity in top positions for fostering inclusivity and maximizing industry potential.

news.headline: Ascent Hospitality chooses Toast to power growing restaurant portfolio including Perkins and Huddle House.
news.summary: Ascent Hospitality, the parent company of popular restaurant chains Perkins and Huddle House, has chosen Toast to support its expanding portfolio of eateries. The decision, announced in Boston, will see Toast provide the necessary technology solutions to enhance operations across Ascent Hospitality's restaurants. This move signifies a major step in the company's growth strategy, as it aims to streamline processes and boost efficiency. This partnership underscores the importance of innovative tools in the competitive restaurant industry and highlights Toast's reputation as a preferred technology provider.

news.headline: PublicSquare partners with Guns.com for ACH processing, bringing potential annual GMV of hundreds of million dollars.
news.summary: A company known as PSQ Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: PSQH) recently introduced Automated Clearing House (ACH) processing capability in conjunction with its payment technology, PSQ Payments. The launch partner for ACH is Guns.com, contributing a significant potential annualized payment processing Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) of several hundred million dollars to the platform. Guns.com had to find a new ACH provider after their previous one unexpectedly terminated services.

news.headline: Establishment Labs launches Preservé™ in Brazil, expanding its minimally invasive platform for breast aesthetics. $ESTA.
news.summary: In New York, Establishment Labs Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: ESTA), a company focusing on women's health in breast aesthetics and reconstruction, has introduced Preservé™ in Brazil, marking the initial step in its worldwide launch. Preservé, the second product in the minimally invasive platform following Mia Femtech®, showcases the technologies and techniques created by Establishment Labs.

news.headline: President wants to raise taxes on private equity profits to fund campaign promises like eliminating taxes on overtime, tipped income, and Social Security benefits.
news.summary: The proposal aims to increase taxes on private equity gains to fund campaign commitments such as removing taxes on overtime pay, tipped earnings, and Social Security benefits. The president is seeking to balance by leveraging a marginal tax rate increase with the objective of fulfilling populist promises.

news.headline: Franklin Electric reports Q4 2024 sales at $485.7M, YoY increase. Full Year 2024 net sales at $2.0B with operating income of $243.6M. EPS $3.86. Cash balance $220.5M. Sales guidance for 2025 at $2.09B-$2.15B, EPS $4.05-$4.25. Operating income Q4 2024 $43.0M. Energy Systems sales up 5%, Water Systems flat. Distribution sales saw 6% increase to $157.2M. Energy Systems operating income was record $24.7M. Cash flow from operating activities for 2024 was at $261.4 million. The earnings call will commence at 9:00 am ET. CEO optimistic about future revenue growth and margin expansion. Resilient balance sheet to capitalize on opportunities in 2025.
news.summary: Franklin Electric Co., Inc. reported fourth quarter 2024 net sales of $485.7 million, a 3% increase from the prior year. Operating income was $43.0 million, with a margin of 8.9%. Full year 2024 net sales decreased by 2% to $2.0 billion. The company had an operating income of $243.6 million and fully diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $3.86. Cash flows from operating activities were $261.4 million, and they ended the year with a cash balance of $220.5 million. The company expects full year 2025 sales to be between $2.09 billion and $2.15 billion, with EPS ranging from $4.05 to $4.25. Water Systems net sales were flat, Distribution net sales increased by 6%, and Energy Systems net sales increased by 5% in the fourth quarter. Operating income for the segments varied. The company's focus for 2025 includes revenue growth and margin expansion through innovation and growth acceleration. The earnings conference call is scheduled at 9:00 am ET for further details.

news.headline: Great Lakes had an exceptional year in 2024: $762.7M revenue, $57.3M net income, and $136.0M Adjusted EBITDA. Dredging backlog hit $1.2B.
news.summary: Great Lakes Dredge & Dock Corporation reported a successful 2024, with a fourth quarter net income of $19.7 million and adjusted EBITDA of $40.2 million. The full-year net income was $57.3 million with adjusted EBITDA of $136.0 million. Revenue for the fourth quarter was $202.8 million, reaching $762.7 million for the full year. The dredging backlog stood at $1.2 billion at the end of December 31, 2024, with additional low bids and options pending award. The company is focused on expanding its offshore energy business and has made significant progress in fleet enhancement and strategic growth initiatives. Great Lakes is optimistic about future opportunities in the dredging industry due to strong government support and market growth projections. Adjusted EBITDA, a key financial metric used by the company, excludes certain expenses to evaluate operating performance. The Company employs about 200 specialized vessels and operates with a focus on safety through its Incident-and-Injury-Free® (IIF®) safety management program.

news.headline: Redfin: Less than one-third of US home purchases were made with cash in 2024, a 3-year low.
South Korea joins AI data center race with plans for up to $35 billion facility, investors say - WSJ.
US engaging in an ongoing trade war poses threat to global economy -Bloomberg.
Secured Overnight Financing Rate unchanged 4.33% Feb. 14th.
US and Russia conduct talks on Ukraine situation. No further details provided.
Tesla launching Model Y in China this month. BYD surges on self-driving efforts. Investor interest peaks.
news.summary: China deliveries of the new Model Y from Tesla are set to commence this month. Meanwhile, the EV company BYD has experienced a significant increase in its stock value due to its focus on self-driving technology. This surge in BYD's stocks comes as Tesla accelerates the release of the Model Y. The competition between Tesla and BYD in the electric vehicle market is intensifying, with both companies making strategic moves to stay ahead.Tesla's new Model Y will cater to the growing demand for electric vehicles in China, with expectations of impressive sales numbers. At the same time, BYD's advancements in self-driving technology have caught the attention of investors, leading to a surge in the company's stock prices.

news.headline: Constellation Energy's earnings rise by 40%, S&P 500 nuclear giant not concerned about DeepSeek's future profit.
news.summary: Earnings for Constellation Energy surged by 40%, exceeding expectations with early results. The S&P 500 nuclear company is unconcerned about DeepSeek and its impact on profits by 2025. This positive development for the giant in the nuclear sector has been surprising for many investors.

news.headline: Cybersecurity stocks rank 18th out of 197 industry groups, Cloudflare, CrowdStrike, and Fortinet among gainers.
news.summary: In a list of 197 IBD industry groups, cybersecurity stocks have claimed the No. 18 spot, with companies like Cloudflare, CrowdStrike, and Fortinet showing notable gains. As we enter 2025, the cybersecurity sector is off to a strong start, promising more earnings in the near future.

news.headline: Egypt and Qatar succeed in ensuring remaining Israeli hostages and Palestinian prisoners to be released on Saturday - Egypt's al Qahera News TV.
Round of discussions on phase 2 of Gaza ceasefire deal successfully concluded - Egypt's al Qahera News TV

Thailand Data Center Market valued at USD 1.56 billion in 2024, estimated to reach USD 3.19 billion by 2030, with 30 operational colocation data centers. Multiple new entrants in the market - GDS Services, Equinix, Evolution Data Centres, CtrlS Datacenters, Edgnex Data Centers by DAMAC, Empyrion Digital and CloudHQ.
news.summary: Dublin - A report on the Thailand Data Center Market is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. The market was worth USD 1.56 billion in 2024 and is anticipated to hit USD 3.19 billion by 2030, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 12.66%. Notably, Thailand currently has more than 30 active colocation data centers. The country is expected to experience substantial expansion in this sector, leading to a rise in the number of data centers.

news.headline: US and Russia peace proposal would include Ukraine elections - Fox.
Vietnam data center market to reach $1.75 trillion by 2030 with a CAGR of 17.93%. In 2024, valued at $654 million.
news.summary: Dublin- A recent report titled "Vietnam Data Center Market - Investment Analysis & Growth Opportunities 2025-2030" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. In 2024, the Vietnamese Data Center Market had a value of USD 654 million and is estimated to escalate to USD 1.75 trillion by 2030, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 17.93%. Noteworthy events in the sector in 2024 include the introduction of new players like Epsilon Telecommunication (KT Corporation), Gaw Capital, and Infracrowd Capital.

news.headline: US loyalty programs market expected to grow by 15.7% annually, reach $27.26 billion in 2025 - ResearchAndMarkets.com.
news.summary: A recent report titled "United States Loyalty Programs Market Intelligence and Future Growth Dynamics" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The loyalty market in the United States is projected to increase by 15.7% annually, reaching US$27.26 billion in 2025. Over the years, the U.S. loyalty market has shown consistent growth, with a recorded CAGR of 17 in value terms. This report provides insights into loyalty programs trends across various sectors, operational KPIs, retail product dynamics, and consumer demographics as of Q1 2025.

news.headline: Payfinia partners with TAPP Engine to offer instant payment services to financial institutions and wealth management providers.
news.summary: In Portland, Oregon, Payfinia has collaborated with TAPP Engine to provide a comprehensive range of immediate payment solutions to financial institutions and wealth management providers through embedded finance. This partnership aims to enhance the efficiency of processing transactions and enabling quick payments.

news.headline: SRA Watchtower secures $4 million funding from FINTOP Capital, JAM FINTOP, EJF Capital after acquiring Lumio Insight.
news.summary: GLEN ALLEN, Va.-- SRA Watchtower has recently secured $4 million in funding, following its acquisition of Lumio Insight. This new investment marks a significant milestone for the company as it continues to expand and strengthen its presence in the compliance industry.

news.headline: EU Commission will promote more flexible gas storage refilling and including with dynamic targets - Draft EU clean industrial deal
EU Commission to launch consultation into whether EU gas market legislation needs changing to ensure regulatory oversight - Draft EU clean industrial deal.
Baidu's Q4 revenue at $4.68B, Core revenue up 1%, Online marketing down 7%. AI Cloud revenue grows 26%. EPS beats estimates.
news.summary: Recently, Baidu, Inc (NASDAQ: BIDU) announced its fiscal fourth-quarter revenue of $4.68 billion, a 2% decrease from the previous year but surpassing the analyst consensus of $4.56 billion. The company's adjusted earnings per ADS were $2.63, exceeding the analyst estimate of $1.78. Despite this positive performance, the stock price experienced a decline following the report. Within its segments, Baidu's Core revenue increased by 1% to $3.8 billion, while Online marketing revenue dropped by 7% to $2.46 billion. Non-online marketing revenue grew by 18% to $1.34billion, fueled by the AI Cloud business which expanded by 26% in the quarter. Additionally, revenue from IQIYI, Inc (NASDAQ: IQ) saw a 14% decrease year over year, amounting to $906 million. For further details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Russia's Dmitriev: Russian and US officials have separate discussion on future economic cooperation, including global energy prices.
BHP saw a 23% drop in earnings, reporting $5.08 billion for the half-year ending December 31, 2024, due to iron ore challenges. Dividend cut to $0.50.
news.summary: BHP reported half-year earnings of $5.08 billion, down 23% from the previous year, as the iron ore market faced challenges. Despite this, CEO Mike Henry noted the firm's operational resilience. Iron ore EBITDA fell from $9.7 billion to $7.2 billion due to a 22% decline in realized prices to $81.11 per ton. Production increased by 1% to 131 million tons on an equity basis, but revenue dropped by 7.5% to $25.176 billion. The board declared an interim dividend of $0.50 per share, lower than last year's $0.72 per share.

news.headline: Waystar reports strong results for Q4 2024 and FY 2024. FY 2024 revenue: $944M (up 19% YoY), Q4 revenue growth: 18% YoY, Q4 net income: $19.1M. Adjusted EBITDA FY 2024: $383M (up 15% YoY).urlpatterns: 100.characters.
news.summary: Waystar Holding Corp. reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024, with a revenue of $944 million for the fiscal year, marking a 19% increase year-over-year. In Q4, revenue saw an 18% growth compared to the previous year. Q4 net income was $19.1 million, while non-GAAP net income was $52.1 million. The company reported a net loss of $19.1 million for the fiscal year, representing a 62.7% improvement year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal year 2024 was $383 million, up 15% YoY. The company provided guidance for fiscal year 2025, expecting total revenue between $1.0 billion and $1.016 billion, adjusted EBITDA between $399 million and $407 million, non-GAAP net income between $237 million and $243 million, and diluted non-GAAP net income per share between $1.29 and $1.32. The company's financial results will be discussed in a conference call, and interested parties can access the webcast on Waystar's website. Non-GAAP financial measures were included to supplement GAAP statements for a comprehensive financial performance analysis.

news.headline: Blackbaud reports 2024 Q4 results: GAAP revenue $302.2M, non-GAAP recurring revenue $296.2M, GAAP loss $330.8M, and aims to become Rule of 45 company by 2030. Full-year 2024 GAAP revenue $1.2B, non-GAAP EBITDA $388.9M, net cash flow $228.8M. 2025 outlook includes revenue $1.115B - $1.125B, EBITDA margin 34.9% - 35.9%. Non-GAAP EPS $4.16 - $4.35, adjusted free cash flow $185M - $195M. Stock repurchase program remains with $645M remaining. Visit Blackbaud's site for more info.
news.summary: Blackbaud, a software provider for social impact, reported positive financial results for Q4 and full-year 2024. In Q4, GAAP total revenue was $302.2 million, up 2.4%, with GAAP recurring revenue at $296.2 million, up 3.1%. Non-GAAP net income was $54.4 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $102.2 million. For the full year, total revenue was $1.2 billion, up 4.5%, with GAAP recurring revenue at $1.1 billion, up 5.4%. Non-GAAP net income was $210.7 million. The company also provided 2025 financial guidance, including revenue of $1.115 billion to $1.125 billion and adjusted EBITDA margin of 34.9% to 35.9%. Blackbaud marked several milestones, including divesting EVERFI and announcing partnerships for social impact. For more details, visit www.blackbaud.com/newsroom.

news.headline: Vulcan Materials reports Q4 and full year 2024 results with total revenues at $7.4B, Adjusted EBITDA at $2.1B, and net earnings at $912M. CEO expects double-digit earnings growth in 2025. Market outlook remains positive with projections for volume growth, price improvements, and cost management strategies. Acquisition and capital allocation activities continue to support growth. Management anticipates strong performance in the coming year. ((*Word count: 98*))
news.summary: Vulcan Materials Company, the largest construction aggregates producer in the US, reported financial results for Q4 2024. Total revenues for the quarter were $1.854 billion, with a gross profit of $537 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $550 million, with a margin of 29.7%. Net earnings attributable to Vulcan were $294 million. The Aggregates segment saw a 15% increase in gross profit, with cash gross profit per ton at $11.50. Vulcan completed strategic acquisitions in 2024 and returned $313 million to shareholders. The outlook for 2025 includes a 19% growth in Adjusted EBITDA, double-digit growth in Aggregates cash gross profit per ton, and other positive projections. Annual revenue was $7.418 billion, with a gross profit of $2 billion. Vulcan's stock is listed on NYSE under the ticker symbol VMC.

news.headline: Cambridge GaN Devices secures $32M for global growth in power semiconductor industry, led by strategic investor.
news.summary: A company based in Cambridge, England, called Cambridge GaN Devices (CGD), specializing in GaN power devices, has recently concluded a Series C funding round, raising an impressive $32 million. The primary investment came from a strategic investor, with additional contributions from British Patient Capital and continuing support from current investors like Parkwalk, BGF, Cambridge Innovation Capital (CIC), Foresight Group, and IQ Capital. The company's focus on GaN technology is revolutionizing power electronics.

news.headline: Acquisition deal: One Equity Partners to buy Wheeler Fleet Solutions.
news.summary: The latest data reveals that the economy grew by 5% in the last quarter, exceeding expectations of a 3.8% growth. Unemployment has fallen to 4.2%, the lowest rate in ten years. Additionally, inflation remains steady at 2.5%. Experts attribute the strong economic performance to increased consumer spending and a boost in manufacturing output. Analysts predict continued growth in the coming months.

news.headline: Southwest faces first nonvoluntary layoffs after 53 years.
Report projects Spain's data center market value to grow from USD 2.25 billion in 2024 to USD 4.30 billion by 2030 at a CAGR of 11.40%.
news.summary: A report titled "Spain Data Center Market - Investment Analysis & Growth Opportunities 2025-2030" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The data center market in Spain had a value of USD 2.25 billion in 2024 and is forecasted to reach USD 4.30 billion by 2030, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 11.40%. A notable trend in the market is companies obtaining renewable energy for their operations, such as Nabiax powering its data centers with 100% renewable sources.

news.headline: DocGo CEO to speak at TD Cowen Health Care Conference on March 5th, 11:10-11:40 AM ET. Webcast available on DocGo's website. Nasdaq: DCGO.
news.summary: In New York, DocGo Inc. (Nasdaq: DCGO) disclosed that its CEO, Lee Bienstock, will take part in individual meetings at the TD Cowen 45th Annual Health Care Conference on March 5th. Additionally, Bienstock is scheduled to present from 11:10 to 11:40 AM ET on the same day. A webcast of the presentation can be accessed on DocGo's investor relations site at https://ir.docgo.com/. DocGo Inc. is a prominent provider of technology-driven mobile health services.

news.headline: Investors are heavily wagering on a substantial drop in volatility in the market.
AREP Fund IV closes at $309M, exceeding target. Demonstrates continued growth in the real estate investment sector.
news.summary: An institutional real estate fund manager and operating company, American Real Estate Partners (AREP), based in McLean, Virginia, revealed the completion of its fourth real estate GP fund, AREP Strategic Opportunity Fund IV ("Fund IV"). This fund has garnered $309 million in equity commitments, showcasing substantial growth following the close of fundraising for AREP's third investment fund in 2022. The success in capital raising for Fund IV highlights AREP's proficiency in recognizing and molding investment opportunities.

news.headline: AZEK Company acquires Northwest Polymers to expand recycling in Pacific Northwest; based in Oregon. NYSE: AZEK.
news.summary: Chicago-based AZEK Company Inc. (NYSE: AZEK), a top manufacturer of sustainable outdoor living products like TimberTech® Decking and Railing, Versatex®, AZEK Trim®, and StruXure® pergolas, has acquired Northwest Polymers. Northwest Polymers is a renowned company in Oregon specializing in post-industrial and post-commercial plastic recycling. This acquisition marks AZEK's expansion in the recycling sector.

news.headline: Tony James' firm is raised $370M for private equity investments.
news.summary: In the latest update, it has been reported that the number of confirmed cases of the recent viral outbreak has surged to over 10,000, with a significant increase in the number of fatalities, now surpassing 500. Health officials emphasize the importance of following preventive measures such as hand hygiene and wearing face masks to curb the spread of the virus. In response to the escalating situation, authorities are implementing stricter quarantine measures in heavily affected regions. Additionally, efforts are underway to ramp up testing capacity and allocate resources to support the healthcare system in managing the influx of patients. The public is urged to stay informed through reliable sources and to cooperate with health guidelines to mitigate the further spread of the virus.

news.headline: A.R.I. partners with Hypereon Labs in St. Petersburg, FL to drive AI innovation via strategic debt financing.
news.summary: A tech company based in St. Petersburg, Florida, A.R.I., has recently collaborated with Hypereon Labs to enhance AI innovation by securing strategic debt financing. This partnership aims to propel advancements in the field of artificial intelligence and technology. The collaboration is anticipated to not only boost research and development in AI but also enable the companies to leverage their expertise in the sector. This move signals a strong commitment towards technological advancement and signifies the shared vision of both companies to lead in AI innovation.
